The list of vulnerabilities in software and firmware has grown exponentially, with more than 40,000 Common Vulnerability Exposures published in 2024—a 38% increase from 2023—challenging security teams to prioritize and address vulnerabilities effectively.

With this surge, applications that track vulnerabilities relevant to firmware are essential. In conjunction with SBOM monitoring, AMI’s Vulnerability Managing Service (VMS) can map known CVEs to specific components, helping teams quickly identify and mitigate security risks hidden within third-party libraries or dependencies used in firmware.

text_orientation_phone=”center” text_orientation_last_edited=”on|tablet” global_colors_info=”{}”]Discover AMI’s Vulnerability Management Service (VMS) – built to secure Boot and BMC firmware with real-time insights, CVE tracking and compliance-ready intelligence.

VMS is a cloud-based security intelligence platform that provides visibility into open-source and proprietary firmware components, helping firmware developers, OEMs and system integrators identify, assess and respond to vulnerabilities. Search for known vulnerabilities by CVE, security advisory or project name. VMS provides critical metadata, severity ratings and remediation guidance, so teams can swiftly resolve issues and stay compliant with the latest cybersecurity directives and regulations.[/et_pb_text][et_pb_button button_url=”https://9443417.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/9443417/Data_Sheets/Security_Solutions/AMI_Meridian_VMS_Data_Sheet_PUB.pdf” url_new_window=”on” global_colors_info=”{}”]A Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) is a comprehensive inventory of components used to build a software or firmware artifact. This transparency is foundational to firmware security, supply chain risk management and regulatory compliance.

The SBOM report details the firmware components, libraries, tools, utilities and build environment used to create a binary. This can include open-source libraries, AMI packages and silicon vendor IP. Our SBOM utilities support both Aptio (Boot) and MegaRAC (BMC) products, allowing developers to generate spec-compliant SBOM reports for their projects.[/et_pb_text][et_pb_button button_url=”https://9443417.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hubfs/9443417/Data_Sheets/Security_Solutions/AMI_SBOM_Utility_Data_Sheet_PUB.pdf” url_new_window=”on” button_text=”Download SBOM Data Sheet” excerpt.rendered: A Unified and Seamless Approach to Firmware SecurityThe list of vulnerabilities in software and firmware has grown exponentially, with more than 40,000 Common Vulnerability Exposures published in 2024—a 38% increase from 2023—challenging security teams to prioritize and address vulnerabilities effectively. With this surge, applications that track vulnerabilities relevant to firmware are essential. In conjunction with […]
